blurb: UK embodied-AI/self-driving scale-up. Launch coverage is anchored on parliamentary written evidence plus a checked-empty ATS status for policy roles.

hiring.note: Wayve Greenhouse board checked live; 109 jobs returned. The only legal/policy keyword match was a Sunnyvale Commercial Legal Counsel role, so no London policy role was added.

publications.title: Wayve — written evidence (ACT0047)
publications.summary: Wayve's written submission to the House of Lords Communications and Digital Committee inquiry 'Scaling up: AI and creative tech' positions it as a UK embodied-AI scale-up and argues for compute access, AV regulatory clarity and AI talent retention.

publications.title: A Global Regulatory Breakthrough for Assisted and Automated Driving
publications.summary: Wayve says it helped shape UNECE globally aligned regulatory pathways and safety standards for assisted and automated driving systems.

publications.title: Driving innovation – 38,000 jobs on the horizon as pilots of self-driving vehicles fast-tracked
publications.summary: DfT announced accelerated UK self-driving pilots under the Automated Vehicles Act 2024 and quoted Wayve’s L4 trial plans with Uber.

publications.title: Government and Wayve sign partnership to accelerate Britain’s self driving future
publications.summary: DBT announced a memorandum of understanding with Wayve to support autonomous-vehicle research and UK commercial deployment.

notes:
launch_harvest 2026-06-27: Parliament URL is primary/named source but HTTP fetch returned 403/Cloudflare; status=needs_browser for full-body recapture. Roles ATS Greenhouse board wayve verified reachable with 119 jobs; status=checked_empty for relevant London policy/legal/governance roles.

2026-07-02 urgent data-quality pass: added Wayve first-party UNECE policy post plus GOV.UK DfT/DBT records and company L4 trials press release; dates verified from page metadata. Roles remain checked-empty for London policy/legal/governance.

news.title: Wayve and Uber Partner to Launch L4 Autonomy Trials in the UK
news.summary: Wayve and Uber announced UK public-road L4 autonomy trials working with the UK Government and Transport for London on permitting.
